description: Use when starting a new project from scratch, scoping what to build before opening Claude Code, planning the prep / architecture / build / harden / ship sequence, sizing a feature for one session, choosing how to break a too-big feature, or deciding whether the current scope fits in one terminal. Routes to chapters 05 (the 7-stage workflow) and 06 (feature sizing) of claude-spine.

results.query: I'm about to start a new MVP — an invoicing tool for Dutch freelancers, ~3 weeks budget. What's the prep → architecture → build → harden → ship sequence I should follow?
this 'add Stripe Billing portal' task feels too big for one session — how do I cut it into the right slices without making 6 PRs?
before I open Claude Code, walk me through scoping this feature: multi-tenant team invites with role-based permissions. Is this one session or does it need to be split?
I've got the schema and 3 API routes done. Where am I in the 7-stage workflow — should I be hardening already or still in build?
the GitHub Actions workflow is failing on the deploy step — fix the YAML
I want to add SSO + multi-region replication + a redesigned dashboard. Is that one feature or three?
the daily-digest cron workflow is firing twice — debug the scheduler
implement an n8n workflow for syncing Stripe customers to HubSpot
explain how Next.js handles the middleware → route handler workflow
design the workflow for user onboarding — verify email, set password, create org, invite team
